segments.text: At this my heart trembles, beating rapidly within me! [^256]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Listen carefully to God's thunderous voice that rumbles as he speaks.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He sends it across the heaven; his lightning flashes to the ends of the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then comes the roar of thunder, his majestic voice holding nothing back when he speaks.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God's thunderous voice is wonderful! We can't comprehend the great things he does!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He tells the snow to fall and the rain to pour down on the earth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: By this he stops people working so that everyone can understand what he does.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even the animals take shelter and remain in their dens.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The south wind blows in storms, while the north wind blows in cold weather. [^257]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God's breath produces ice, freezing the surface of water solid.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He fills the clouds with moisture; he scatters his lightning from them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They swirl around under his control; they move over all the earth as he commands.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He does this to accomplish his will, either to discipline or to show his goodness. [^258]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Listen to this, Job! Stop for a moment and consider the wonderful things God does.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Do you know how God controls the clouds, or makes his lightning flash from them?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Do you know how clouds float[^259] in the sky—the wonderful work of him who knows everything.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You know how your clothes drip with sweat when the south wind brings air that is hot and heavy.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Can you hammer out the sky so it becomes like a molten mirror, as he does?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So why don't you teach us what to tell God? We cannot make our case because we are in the dark! [^260]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Should God be told that I want to speak? Anyone who wanted to would be destroyed! [^261]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: After all, we can't look at the sun when it blazes brightly in the sky, after the wind has cleared away the clouds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Out of the north God comes shining like gold, surrounded in awesome majesty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We cannot approach the Almighty for he is far beyond us in power and justice, and doing right.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He does not act like a tyrant—no wonder people are in awe of him, though he does not value those who think they're wise.
segments.speaker: Unknown
